message:
fix(buf,postgres,http,web,dns,quic,messaging): Limit bounds, negotiate_encoding signature, PG message overflow, DNS cache TTL, and 6 other targeted fixes

src/bytes/buf/limit.rs:
- Fix Limit::chunk_mut() to clamp against remaining_mut() (min of limit
  and inner remaining) instead of just limit, preventing oversized slices
  when limit exceeds the inner buffer's actual capacity
- Fix advance_mut() and put_slice() to validate against remaining_mut()
  instead of just limit, matching Take::advance() semantics
- Add assertion in chunk_mut() that non-zero remaining implies non-empty
  chunk (fail-closed on broken BufMut implementations)
- Add 4 new tests: limit_exceeds_inner for advance_mut and put_slice,
  panics_when_count_exceeds_effective, chunk_mut_zero_inner_panics

src/database/postgres.rs:
- Change build_message() and build_startup_message() from panic on
  i32 overflow to returning Result<Vec<u8>, PgError>, propagating the
  error through ? instead of expect(), preventing panics on pathological
  >2GiB payloads
- Update all call sites to use ? propagation

src/http/compress.rs:
- Change negotiate_encoding() signature from &str to Option<&str>,
  distinguishing absent Accept-Encoding (server preference + identity
  fallback) from empty Accept-Encoding (identity only)
- Update doc comments and all test call sites

src/web/compress.rs + src/web/middleware.rs:
- Update negotiate_encoding call sites to pass Option<&str>
- Add content-type aware compression bypass for already-compressed
  media types

src/web/multipart.rs:
- Minor formatting fix

src/net/dns/cache.rs:
- Add TTL floor enforcement in cache insertion to prevent zero-TTL
  entries from polluting the cache indefinitely
- Add test for zero-TTL rejection

src/net/quic_native/transport.rs:
- Formatting cleanup for PTO calculation

src/messaging/service.rs:
- Add service request validation helpers with structured error context

src/messaging/stream.rs:
- Add stream config validation with structured error types

src/actor.rs:
- Fix format!("{:?}", v) → format!("{v:?}") for clippy compliance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
